mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns do not significantly exceed baseline when FEDFUNDS declines for three consecutive months (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5 percent, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending and corporate revenue, driving negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive or neutral in over 50% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and housing starts indicates a synchronized demand-supply shock that erodes profit margins and triggers equity sell-offs within a week.
failure_condition: Rank IC between the joint INDPRO-HOUST contraction signal and SPX 5-day returns remains below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, accelerating real GDP growth fails to support equity valuations because margin compression dominates, producing negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower when GDPC1 growth accelerates while CPIAUCSL remains above 315.0.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic expansion that sustains corporate earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for PAYEMS month-over-month changes against SPX 5-day returns falls below 0.02 over a 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds the prevailing CPI inflation rate by more than 100 basis points, real borrowing costs turn restrictive, compressing equity multiples and driving negative returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns do not exhibit statistically significant negative skew when the real rate threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling real personal savings indicates inventory-driven demand that precedes a demand collapse, triggering negative equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and falling RSAFS fails to predict negative SPX 60-day returns in more than 40% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid housing market cooling, declining housing starts lead consumer discretionary spending, which suppresses corporate earnings and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns remain positive or neutral in over 50% of cases where HOUST contracts for two consecutive months.
